human_verification.notes: Validated physician-office revenue boundaries, CMS and HHS grounded workflow sources, denial and override handling, validated packaging, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to medical-office revenue intake, claim support, posting, reconciliation, and denial-exception handling rather than generic accounts receivable, coding, privacy, compliance, or accounting ownership.
Authority sources anchor the pack in CMS professional-claim, remittance, and denial workflow guidance, OIG physician-practice compliance guidance, and HHS OCR minimum-necessary privacy guidance.

Scenarios validate unsupported charge requests, missing documentation, ERA and ledger mismatches, denial escalation, generic-industry overreach, unsupported financial overrides, and multisite tenant adaptation.

The package requires named medical-office records such as encounter notes, superbills, CMS-1500 or 837P work items, claim-status responses, ERA or EOB detail, patient-ledger records, and approval matrices before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of EHR, practice-management, clearinghouse, payer, remittance, and approval records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
